name: Noelia Castillo Ramos
summary: Survived multiple sexual assaults including a gang rape. After attempting suicide and becoming permanently paraplegic, she fought for 601 days for the right to euthanasia.
story:
Noelia Castillo Ramos was a 25-year-old woman from Barcelona who endured repeated sexual violence. From the age of 13, she was under the guardianship of the Catalan government, growing up in institutions. She survived an attempted assault by a partner, an attempted assault by two men at a nightclub, and a gang rape by an ex-boyfriend and three other men — which she described as the definitive breaking point.

On October 4, 2022, days after the final assault, Noelia attempted to end her life by jumping from a fifth-floor window. She survived but suffered a complete, irreversible spinal cord injury that left her permanently paraplegic with severe chronic pain.

In 2024, she requested euthanasia under Spanish law. Her father, supported by a Christian lawyers' organization, fought to block it through every level of the Spanish courts and the European Court of Human Rights. Every court upheld her right to choose.

On March 26, 2026, after 601 days of waiting, Noelia received euthanasia. Her aggressors were never convicted.

In her final interview, she said: "Quiero dejar de sufrir e irme en paz" — "I want to stop suffering and go in peace."

name: Gisèle Pelicot
summary: Drugged by her husband for nearly a decade and raped by 72 strangers he recruited online. She waived her anonymity to demand a public trial, becoming a global symbol of courage.
story:
Over nearly a decade from 2011 to 2020, Dominique Pelicot drugged his wife Gisèle with sedatives, rendering her unconscious. He then raped her himself and recruited strangers through online forums to come to their home and assault her. Gisèle was raped at least 92 times by 72 different men. She was entirely unaware throughout.

The crimes were discovered in September 2020 when Dominique was arrested at a supermarket for taking upskirt photographs. Police found hundreds of images and videos on his devices documenting years of assaults.

The trial began September 2, 2024, in Avignon. Gisèle made the extraordinary decision to waive her right to anonymity and demand a public trial so that, in her words, "shame changes sides." Her courage drew worldwide attention and sparked a national reckoning in France over consent laws.

On December 19, 2024, all 51 defendants were found guilty. Dominique Pelicot received the maximum sentence of 20 years. The 50 co-defendants received 3 to 15 years.
